title: Dallas and Houston Robotaxi Fleets Grow to 2 Vehicles Each After Launch
summary: Crowdsourced Robotaxi Tracker data shows Tesla added a second unsupervised vehicle in both Dallas and Houston shortly after launch, totaling four vehicles across the new cities. Austin fleet logged 13 unsupervised vehicles active in the last 30 days.
impact_reason: Initial fleet additions in new cities and recent Austin activity indicate mild positive momentum in deploying more unsupervised vehicles, supporting gradual scaling.

title: Tesla Unsupervised Robotaxi Fleet Reaches 10 Vehicles in Austin
summary: Tesla's Austin robotaxi fleet hit 10 unsupervised Model Y vehicles, marking the highest confirmed count for driverless service in the city. This reflects ongoing ramp-up in the primary operational hub.
impact_reason: Confirmed growth to 10 unsupervised vehicles in Austin signals clear progress in fleet size expansion, a key gating factor for scaling velocity in the core Texas market.

title: Tesla Discloses 14 Crashes Involving Austin Robotaxis Since Launch
summary: In a February filing, Tesla reported 14 crashes involving its Austin robotaxis since service launch. No further details on severity or regulatory impact provided.
impact_reason: Disclosure of multiple crashes in Austin introduces mild safety concerns that could prompt scrutiny or delays, potentially hindering fleet scaling velocity.
